name: contxt-sdk
documentation:
# Contxt Python SDK

_the Python SDK for Contxt_

[![CI](https://github.com/ndustrialio/contxt-sdk-python/workflows/CI/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ndustrialio/contxt-sdk-python/actions?query=workflow%3ACI)
[![pypi version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/contxt-sdk.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/contxt-sdk/)
![python](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.9+-blue.svg)

## Installation

```sh
pip install contxt-sdk
```

## CLI Usage

```sh
contxt --help
```

## Tests

```sh
docker compose up -d --wait
poetry run poe test
docker compose down
```

## Documentation

Please refer to <https://ndustrialio.github.io/contxt-sdk-python>.

## Contributing

Please refer to [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

classes.name: contxt.models.etl.ChangeTrackingRecord
documentation:
A JSON Serializable ndustrial.io ChangeTrackingRecord(tm) that is
ingestable by the ndustrial ETL java/kotlin components.

topic: Str           - the name of the canonical being ETL-ed
version: Str         - the change table version or CDC version number (cursor position)
operation: enum      - a DB operation (insert/update/upsert/delete) from the DBOperation enum
source_host: Str     - the hostname/ip/table that homes the source data
source_table: Str    - the name of the table from where the data is being extracted
source_database: Str - the name of the db being extracted against
tuple: dict          - key/value pairs that make up the data payload. This
                       value defaults to an empty dict and can be updated after creation
headers: CTRHeader() - the header information that informs ETL devices what do with the CTR

classes.name: contxt.auth.TokenProvider

methods.name: contxt.auth.TokenProvider._token_expiring
documentation:
Returns if `access_token` is within `within` seconds of expiring.
Returns `False` if `exp` is not in the token's claims.

methods.name: contxt.auth.TokenProvider.access_token
documentation: Gets a valid access token for audience `audience`

methods.name: contxt.auth.TokenProvider.access_token
documentation: Sets both the access token and the decoded access token

An abstact base class to provide an unexpired `access_token` from the source
client to the target client, defined by `audience`.

Overload this class to implement `access_token`.

classes.name: contxt.auth.Auth
documentation:
An abstract base class for a client defined by `client_id` and `client_secret`.
To authenticate to another client, use `get_token_provider()`.

Overload this class to implement `get_token_provider()`.

classes.name: contxt.auth.machine.MachineTokenProvider

methods.name: contxt.auth.machine.MachineTokenProvider.access_token
documentation: Gets a valid access token for audience `audience`

Concrete `TokenProvider` for a machine client, where `client_id` and
`client_secret` serve as the identity provider.

classes.name: contxt.auth.cli.UserIdentityProvider

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.UserIdentityProvider.access_token
documentation: Gets a valid access token for audience `audience`

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.UserIdentityProvider.login
documentation:
Returns an access_token from Auth0 after running the
"Device Authorization Flow" successfully

Concrete `TokenProvider` for a user's identity. The access token is provided
directly from Auth0 via username and password and granted for offline access, so
expired tokens are refreshed with a refresh token.

If `cache_file` is specified, both the `access_token` and `refresh_token`
are cached there as a JSON blob.

classes.name: contxt.auth.cli.UserTokenProvider

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.UserTokenProvider.access_token
documentation: Gets a valid access token for audience `audience`

Concrete `TokenProvider` for a user, where `identity_provider` serves as the
identity provider.

classes.name: contxt.auth.cli.CliAuth

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.CliAuth.login
documentation: Force a prompt for the user to login. Note this will happen automatically.

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.CliAuth.logout
documentation: Logs the user out by clearing the cache

methods.name: contxt.auth.cli.CliAuth.query_user
documentation: Query the user with `question`, and return if user confirmed

Concrete `Auth` for a CLI user, where `identity_provider` authenticates requests
for an access token for target clients defined by `audience`.

The access token from Auth0 is cached in a JSON file in a hidden directory,
meaning that the user will only have to authenticate with their credientials
once.

Auth Flow:
1. User provides Contxt username/password credentials
2. Retrieve access token directly from Auth0 for our `AuthService`, authenticating
   with above credientials
3. When authenticating to a service, retrieve access token from our `AuthService`
   for the target service, authenticating with the above Auth0 access token

functions.name: contxt.cli.utils.pluck
documentation:
Pluck `keys` from `items`. Nested keys can be specified by concatenating with
`key_sep` (i.e. `key1.key2`). Returned keys are set via `key_func`.

Create facilities from a CSV file, containing the columns:

 
- name: string
- timezone_name: IANA timezone database name (ex: America/New_York)

functions.name: contxt.cli.commands.auth.token

methods.name: contxt.services.ems.EmsService.get_monthly_utility_spend
documentation:
Get monthly utility spend for facility `facility_id` and resource
`resource_type` (for now, this must be "electric" but will be expanded).
Note `start_date` defaults to 10 years ago (the API's maximum limit) and
`end_date` defaults to today.

methods.name: contxt.services.ems.EmsService.get_monthly_utility_usage
documentation:
Get monthly utility usage for facility `facility_id` and resource
`resource_type` (for now, this must be "electric" but will be expanded).
Note `start_date` defaults to 10 years ago (the API's maximum limit) and
`end_date` defaults to today.

methods.name: contxt.services.ems.EmsService.get_usage
documentation: Get utility usage for facility `facility_id` and resource `resource_type`

methods.name: contxt.services.ngest.NgestService.send_time_series
documentation:
Sends time series data for field(s).

```
param feed_key

feed's key

str
```

methods.name: contxt.services.api.Api.raw_post

An API with url `base_url`.

If `token_provider` is specified, all requests will be authenticated with

classes.name: contxt.services.api.ApiEnvironment
documentation:
An environment for an API.

Note `client_id` is only needed if authentication is required.

classes.name: contxt.services.api.ConfiguredApi
documentation:
An `Api` configured for multiple environments, such as staging and production.
Available environments are expressed by `_envs` and the desired environment is
specified by its name `env`.

Overload this class to implement `_envs`.

documentation: Get logger with name `name` and level `level`

classes.name: contxt.utils.cachedproperty
documentation:
The `cachedproperty` is used similar to {class}`property`, except
that the wrapped method is only called once. This is commonly used
to implement lazy attributes.

After the property has been accessed, the value is stored on the
instance itself, using the same name as the cachedproperty. This
allows the cache to be cleared with {func}`delattr`, or through
manipulating the object's `__dict__`.

Terminology

classes.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer

methods.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer.to_dict
documentation:
Serializes `obj` to a `dict`. To use a custom format, overload
`obj.to_dict()`.

```
param obj

object to serialize

type obj

Any

param cls_key

if provided, set dict[key] = , defaults to None

param cls_key

str, optional

param key_filter

callable filter to keep a subset of keys, of the form
```

f(key) = keep?, defaults to ignore "hidden" variables (i.e. starting with "\_")
\:param key_filter: bool, optional
\:return: dictionary
\:rtype: `dict`

methods.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer.to_json
documentation:
Serializes `obj` to JSON.

```
param obj

object to serialize

type obj

Any

param path

path for dumping, defaults to None

param path

Path, optional

return

JSON

rtype

str
```

methods.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer.to_table
documentation:
Serializes `obj` to a table.

```
param obj

object to serialize

type obj

Any

param path

path for dumping, defaults to None

param path

Path, optional

return

table

rtype

tabulate
```

methods.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer.to_csv
documentation:
Serializes `obj` to CSV.

```
param obj

object to serialize

type obj

Any

param path

path to export

type path

Path

param header

write header row, defaults to True

param header

bool, optional
```

methods.name: contxt.utils.serializer.Serializer.to_file
documentation:
Write an object to a file (or stdout).

If path is not provided, `obj` is sent to stdout. Otherwise, the file
extension is used to determine the data format of `obj` before exporting.
Supported file extensions are csv (calls `Serializer.to_csv`),
json (calls `Serializer.to_json`), or txt (calls `Serializer.to_table`).

```
param obj

object to output

type obj

Any

param path

path to export, defaults to None

param path

Optional[Path], optional

param valid_exts

supported file extensions, defaults to
```

(".csv", ".json", ".txt")
\:param valid_exts: Optional\[List[str]\], optional
Serializer to transform a Python object to common data formats
